blurb: Protects and manages Alberta's forests, wildfire response, and provincial parks — overseeing sustainable forest management, timber allocation, wildfire prevention and suppression, and outdoor recreation on public lands.
_status: COMPLETE — Pass 2. 3 goals, 29 supporting services, 0 ministry foundational, 6 goal-level foundational. All 29 services have legislation tags, reach estimates, and economic_impact. Channel audit added 6 phone channels. User group audit expanded 7 services.

goals.title: OUTCOME 1: Alberta's forests are sustainably managed and support a responsible forest products sector
goals.description: The ministry manages Alberta's forests as a long-term public resource — setting management standards, certifying forestry professionals, training the workforce, allocating timber harvesting rights through the forest tenure system, collecting Crown fees and dues, and supporting Alberta's forest business and trade access.

foundational_services.name: Monitor and manage forest health threats
foundational_services.desc: Surveillance and management of mountain pine beetle, spruce budworm, and other forest pests and diseases across Alberta's forests.

mandate_tags.tip: Complete the active forestry management plan for forest health, biodiversity, and carbon sequestration

foundational_services.name: Manage reforestation and seed programs
foundational_services.desc: Tree improvement, seed collection, genetic resource management, and reforestation standards to maintain forest renewal after harvest or disturbance.

mandate_tags.tip: Complete the active forestry management plan for forest health, biodiversity, and carbon sequestration

foundational_services.name: Conduct prescribed burns for forest management
foundational_services.desc: Planned and controlled burns to reduce fuel loads, maintain wildlife habitat, and promote healthy, resilient forests.

mandate_tags.tip: Advance wildfire mitigation through proactive harvesting, public education, and expanded FireSmart/fireguard programs

supporting.name: Access forest management standards and guidelines
supporting.desc: Plans, guidelines, and standards for sustainable forest management in Alberta, including the Forest Management Planning Standard.
supporting.users: Forest industry operators, professionals, and researchers

mandate_tags.tip: Complete the active forestry management plan for forest health, biodiversity, and carbon sequestration

economic_impact.note: Underpins $8B/yr forest sector GDP and 28,000 direct jobs

supporting.name: Find a regulated forestry professional
supporting.desc: Professionals regulated under the Regulated Forest Management Profession Act work with government and industry to apply sound forestry principles in Alberta.
supporting.users: Forest industry, landowners, and the public

economic_impact.note: Professional regulation ensures qualified practitioners manage Alberta's ~28M hectares of forest

supporting.name: Get forestry and wildfire training
supporting.desc: Technical and safety training programs for forest and wildfire management, currently delivered at the Hinton Training Centre.
supporting.users: Forest and wildfire workers, industry partners, First Nations communities

mandate_tags.tip: Advance wildfire mitigation through proactive harvesting, public education, and expanded FireSmart/fireguard programs

economic_impact.note: Trains wildfire and forestry workforce (~1,500 seasonal firefighters, ~5,000 forestry workers)

supporting.name: Apply for a timber quota
supporting.desc: Long-term timber harvesting tenure granted under the Forests Act. Timber quotas are one of Alberta's three forest tenure systems.
supporting.users: Forest companies and commercial timber operators

mandate_tags.tip: Complete the active forestry management plan for forest health, biodiversity, and carbon sequestration
Develop a value-added forestry tax incentive program with Jobs, Economy, Trade and Immigration

economic_impact.note: Quota holders manage ~80% of Alberta's annual allowable cut; supports mill operations and rural employment

supporting.name: Apply for a timber permit
supporting.desc: Shorter-term commercial timber harvesting authorizations granted under the Forests Act.
supporting.users: Forest companies, small operators, and contractors

mandate_tags.tip: Complete the active forestry management plan for forest health, biodiversity, and carbon sequestration
Develop a value-added forestry tax incentive program with Jobs, Economy, Trade and Immigration

economic_impact.note: Timber permits support sawmill supply chains and rural economic activity

supporting.name: Pay timber dues and Crown fees
supporting.desc: Fees paid to the Crown for timber harvested under quotas, permits, and forest management agreements.

mandate_tags.tip: Develop a value-added forestry tax incentive program with Jobs, Economy, Trade and Immigration

economic_impact.note: Crown timber revenue (~$200M/yr) funds forest management and renewal

supporting.name: Report timber damage assessment
supporting.desc: Damage assessment process for timber lost to fire, insect, or other causes, used to adjust Crown fees and harvesting plans.

economic_impact.note: TDA tables adjust Crown fees after natural damage; ensures fair timber royalty calculations

supporting.name: Get a personal-use tree cutting permit
supporting.desc: Non-commercial permits for firewood collection, transplants, and Christmas trees on Crown land, including area maps and rules.
supporting.users: Alberta residents collecting firewood or trees for personal use

economic_impact.note: Free firewood and Christmas trees provide household cost savings; ~8,000 families/yr

supporting.name: Access forest business, market, and trade information
supporting.desc: Information on Alberta's forest products sector, markets, and trade access.

mandate_tags.tip: Develop a value-added forestry tax incentive program with Jobs, Economy, Trade and Immigration

economic_impact.note: Supports Alberta's $4B/yr in forest product exports

supporting.name: Access forestry directives and standard operating procedures
supporting.desc: Policy directives and SOPs that provide formal direction for Alberta's forest policy and legislation.

mandate_tags.tip: Complete the active forestry management plan for forest health, biodiversity, and carbon sequestration

economic_impact.note: Operational compliance enables continuous harvest and reforestation cycle

goals.title: OUTCOME 2: Albertans and visitors enjoy accessible, safe outdoor recreation
goals.description: The ministry operates Alberta's provincial parks system (500+ sites, 27,525 km²) and manages recreation access to public lands, including camping, trails, and public land use zones.

foundational_services.name: Maintain and develop park and trail infrastructure
foundational_services.desc: Capital construction, maintenance, and upgrades to campgrounds, trails, day-use areas, and visitor facilities across the provincial parks system.

mandate_tags.tip: Build 900+ new campsites by 2033 (300+ by 2027)
Continue Kananaskis Country trail upgrades, including Highway 742

supporting.name: Plan a visit to a provincial park
supporting.desc: Provincial park locations, rules, events, maps, and planning resources across Alberta's parks and protected areas network.

mandate_tags.tip: Complete the Recreation and Conservation Strategy to expand public access to natural spaces

economic_impact.note: Alberta Parks contribute ~$3.5B/yr in tourism spending

supporting.name: Reserve a campsite
supporting.desc: Reserve campsites, group camping areas, and comfort camping units in Alberta's provincial parks — online or by phone.

mandate_tags.tip: Build 900+ new campsites by 2033 (300+ by 2027)

economic_impact.note: Campground fees and surrounding tourism spending support rural economies

supporting.name: Buy a park conservation pass
supporting.desc: Daily or yearly pass required in designated conservation areas. Currently covers Kananaskis Country and the Bow Valley Corridor; additional areas may be added. Revenue supports conservation.
supporting.users: Visitors to designated park conservation areas

mandate_tags.tip: Expand trails, campsites, and public land use opportunities with Alberta entrepreneurs in high-traffic areas
Continue Kananaskis Country trail upgrades, including Highway 742

economic_impact.note: Conservation pass revenue funds park operations and infrastructure

supporting.name: Check park advisories and closures
supporting.desc: Current advisories, fire bans, public safety notices, and closures in provincial parks.

economic_impact.note: Timely advisories prevent visitor injuries and reduce search-and-rescue incidents

supporting.name: Get a Public Lands Camping Pass
supporting.desc: Required for anyone 18 and older who random camps along the Eastern Slopes of the Rocky Mountains.
supporting.users: Alberta residents and visitors who random camp on Crown land

mandate_tags.tip: Expand trails, campsites, and public land use opportunities with Alberta entrepreneurs in high-traffic areas

economic_impact.note: Pass revenue supports Crown land recreation infrastructure

supporting.name: Find a public land recreation area or trail
supporting.desc: Designated public land recreation areas, trails, and day-use sites across Alberta's Crown land.

mandate_tags.tip: Complete the Recreation and Conservation Strategy to expand public access to natural spaces
Expand trails, campsites, and public land use opportunities with Alberta entrepreneurs in high-traffic areas
Continue Kananaskis Country trail upgrades, including Highway 742

economic_impact.note: Outdoor recreation linked to $3.3B/yr in health cost savings through physical activity

supporting.name: Check Public Land Use Zone rules
supporting.desc: Permitted activities and regulations for recreational use within Public Land Use Zones (PLUZs).

mandate_tags.tip: Complete the Recreation and Conservation Strategy to expand public access to natural spaces

economic_impact.note: Clear rules reduce user conflicts and protect sensitive environmental areas

supporting.name: Check public land closures and advisories
supporting.desc: Current trail, area, and road closures on Crown land.

economic_impact.note: Closure compliance prevents injuries and protects recovering ecosystems

supporting.name: Access outdoor recreation maps and guides
supporting.desc: Downloadable maps, fact sheets, and guides to help plan recreation activities on Alberta's public land.

mandate_tags.tip: Complete the Recreation and Conservation Strategy to expand public access to natural spaces

economic_impact.note: Supports safe recreational use and tourism dispersal across the province

supporting.name: Search Crown land enforcement history
supporting.desc: Look up enforcement records taken against a company or individual by the Lands Division. Shared delivery with Environment and Protected Areas.

economic_impact.note: Transparency tool supports due diligence and public accountability for Crown land use

goals.title: OUTCOME 3: Wildfire management protects communities and forest ecosystems
goals.description: Alberta Wildfire prevents, prepares for, and responds to wildfires in the Forest Protection Area — protecting communities, critical infrastructure, and forest ecosystems.

foundational_services.name: Operate wildfire detection and dispatch systems
foundational_services.desc: Fire lookout towers, aerial detection patrols, lightning detection, and dispatch operations that enable early wildfire detection across the Forest Protection Area.

mandate_tags.tip: Secure a federal wildfire mitigation agreement focused on Banff, Jasper, and Waterton

foundational_services.name: Deploy and manage wildfire suppression resources
foundational_services.desc: Coordination of wildfire crews, air tankers, helicopters, heavy equipment, and incident management teams during active wildfire response.
foundational_services.users: Government wildfire operations staff, mutual aid partners

mandate_tags.tip: Secure a federal wildfire mitigation agreement focused on Banff, Jasper, and Waterton

supporting.name: Get a fire permit
supporting.desc: Required for any burning in the Forest Protection Area from March 1 to October 31, with the exception of a campfire.
supporting.users: Residents, industry, and contractors burning in the Forest Protection Area

relationships.service: Check current fire bans and restrictions

mandate_tags.tip: Advance wildfire mitigation through proactive harvesting, public education, and expanded FireSmart/fireguard programs

economic_impact.note: Regulated burning prevents escaped fires; avg wildfire suppression costs $500M-$1B/yr

supporting.name: Check current fire bans and restrictions
supporting.desc: Live list of fire advisories, restrictions, and bans across Alberta's Forest Protection Area and parks.

mandate_tags.tip: Advance wildfire mitigation through proactive harvesting, public education, and expanded FireSmart/fireguard programs

economic_impact.note: Compliance with bans prevents human-caused wildfires (~40% of all fires)

supporting.name: Practise safe burning
supporting.desc: Guidance on burning debris, agricultural burning, and other controlled burns to reduce wildfire risk.

mandate_tags.tip: Advance wildfire mitigation through proactive harvesting, public education, and expanded FireSmart/fireguard programs

economic_impact.note: Safe burning practices reduce escaped-fire incidents

supporting.name: Prepare your property for wildfire (FireSmart)
supporting.desc: FireSmart Alberta community and homeowner program to reduce wildfire risk to homes, neighbourhoods, and critical infrastructure.

mandate_tags.tip: Advance wildfire mitigation through proactive harvesting, public education, and expanded FireSmart/fireguard programs

economic_impact.note: FireSmart reduces structure loss; 2023 Jasper/Edson fires cost $1B+ in damages

supporting.name: Report a wildfire
supporting.desc: Report a wildfire in Alberta's Forest Protection Area by calling 310-FIRE (310-3473) or using the online form.

mandate_tags.tip: Secure a federal wildfire mitigation agreement focused on Banff, Jasper, and Waterton

economic_impact.note: Early detection dramatically reduces suppression cost and area burned

supporting.name: Check wildfire status
supporting.desc: Live dashboard of current wildfire locations, sizes, status, and response in Alberta.
supporting.users: Alberta residents, visitors, municipalities, and media

mandate_tags.tip: Advance wildfire mitigation through proactive harvesting, public education, and expanded FireSmart/fireguard programs

economic_impact.note: Public awareness enables timely evacuation and reduces emergency response costs

supporting.name: Access wildfire maps and data
supporting.desc: Wildfire maps, historical data, and GIS resources for research, planning, and operational use.
supporting.users: Researchers, municipalities, industry, and the public

relationships.service: Access forest management standards and guidelines

mandate_tags.tip: Advance wildfire mitigation through proactive harvesting, public education, and expanded FireSmart/fireguard programs

economic_impact.note: Data supports municipal emergency planning and insurance risk modelling

supporting.name: Help with wildfire response
supporting.desc: Pathways for individuals and organizations to help during active wildfire response, including contract services and volunteer opportunities.

mandate_tags.tip: Advance wildfire mitigation through proactive harvesting, public education, and expanded FireSmart/fireguard programs

economic_impact.note: Contract crews and volunteers augment provincial capacity during peak fire seasons

supporting.name: Apply to become a wildland firefighter
supporting.desc: Recruitment for Alberta Wildfire's seasonal and permanent wildland firefighting positions.
supporting.users: Job seekers interested in wildland firefighting

mandate_tags.tip: Advance wildfire mitigation through proactive harvesting, public education, and expanded FireSmart/fireguard programs

economic_impact.note: ~1,500 seasonal wildland firefighters deployed annually; critical provincial capacity

cross_cutting: Forest Resource Improvement Association of Alberta (FRIAA)
Three forest tenure systems (FMA, quota, permit)
Eastern Slopes Public Lands Camping Pass
